Toasty.
Toasty.
I can't complain, though.

Yeah, well, stay cool out there.
Yeah.
But yeah, if you have this long to enjoy not over 100, that's good.

Nice.
All right, cool.
Yeah, so happy to chat through some things.
We've got, you know, some new deliverables for you this week.

So that's cruising.
How do you feel about the images?
Talk to me about those, like, the ones that you've seen so far and the ones we've...

think you nailed it with the specs that we had given you.
Okay, cool.
And we can make tweaks in the future.

But for now, I think as long as you like it, we'll keep cruising with that.

I think that works for me.

Chris, do you have context?
Yeah, we're talking about the image, hero, and what the headline should be.

So I, I think the primary way that these images actually act in our, you know, for the, for our purposes is, is really should be to drive reading.
So we're basically trying to reduce the risk of bouncing back to Google.
So I think we should, we should treat that image for that purpose to make you want to read by, I don't know, just, I think that should be enough of a prompt to think about what it's really doing.
Because we're really, and all at the end of the day, we're really just trying to drive a ranking.
And so.
That image can, I think, help us reduce bouncing back without scrolling.
Yeah.
So that is the way that the workflow works natively.
It looks at the body copy, and it says, hey, what would be a good phrase that represents, you know, a core idea in the article?
And then let's put that in title length.
You know, let's rephrase it so that it's title length.
And then puts it out there.
I think we had one weird one, which actually triggered an internal workflow for us, which is good.
It'll benefit everybody internally because we needed to add an override, basically.
It was like, hey, I'd like a custom text box here.
And if I put the title or any other text in that box, that's what ends up in the image.
But natively, the workflow does not do that or take the title.

talking about.
So we can experiment with that.
We can create a batch, like our next batch of articles, we can do with that.

I'm trying to think here.
Let me ponder whether it would be possible for us to generate both and then provide both of those in the air table.
I don't want to add additional complexity to the publishing flow because like then our publishers like, okay, which one did we decide and all that.
But let me let me think about it.
But I can absolutely do our next batch with our with our native flow and you can get a vibe check on it and see how you like it.
I agree with you.
It's an opportunity to pull out a core idea.
And then because usually like in a share card, the title will also be present.
So you'll get the title and you'll get an idea pulled out.
So I generally like to extend.
The surface area, but I always like to defer because sometimes because of the way the site is organized, you want the title to be there.
I think that flexibility would be nice to have your generated title in addition to the title.
I would say that it was for that first couple of batches, it was maybe like 80% were good.
The times when it was off the rails, it was in a couple of cases, it was like way off the rails.
But for the most part, it was good enough.
Okay, cool.
Let's do a batch and see what we get there.

Cool.

For Ramp?
Yeah.
I don't know.
Let me look, actually.
I can tell you right now.
They look good.

I think that's the way that works.
I'm going to look at it right now for you.
Whatever is working for them, and if you need something from us to make it work also for us, let me know, we need to give you brand assets at a certain way with enough flexibility.
I kind of like how RAMP's doing theirs right now.
Okay.
Cool.
Let me look at it here.

Yeah.
Yeah.
Let's see here.
I'm actually going to, I'll just put this in action.
I can't tell internally what we're doing here.
So, or I can't tell from the tool at a glance.
So, I'll figure it out.
They look too good to be done by AI.
So, like, I'm just curious.
You'd be surprised.
It depends, right?
Like, some stylistic elements, you're like, wow, this is, you know, what a great designer.

It's an AI workflow that takes Figma assets and mixes them, you know?
But sometimes it's not.
They may have a designer.
mean, Ramp, you know, may just have a full-time team on it.
So, I'll ask.
And we'll get back to you.
Thank you.
Yep.
Cool.
So, we've got here.
Articles.
Oh, I had one.
There was a Yes.
Stylistic question for you.

If it's being published under Robert's byline, dropping into the first person on a take that would be an authoritative assessment from Robert, as an example, I think is totally fine, but I think it's a stylistic question that you would maybe want to have some input on.

So do you have an opinion on whether it says, like, I believe blah, blah, blah, or, you know, I think I have seen personally, that's the ones that I've seen, where it says, like, I have seen that blah, blah, blah, blah, you know, firms that perform this process have whatever improvements.
Do you want that to happen, not want it to happen, or are agnostic?
And I think that we will continue to move more toward highlighting Robert.

Maybe.
then an internal link to about me, you know, essentially.

Okay.

I'll keep an eye on it and leave it in for now.

I just wanted to get a check on.

And then like we as a firm believe it's a, you know, yada, yada.

Yeah.

And if you're not, all you got to do is let us know.

And so it's organically deciding to use first person when it's basically like an authoritative opinion based segment.
Or even fact driven, but sort of it organically comes from a personal perspective.
It's doing a good job at it, I think.
I just wanted to check on that with you.

me to bring up here is that there was a specific example is like, when I was working on this project, and it made it seem like it was like last month, that he was working on that project.

I just wanted to gut check on that because I didn't.

have not all clients are as smooth as you folks.

weeks.

appreciate it.

after.

again.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync to discuss content strategy, deliverables, and future initiatives for Monograph's marketing efforts.](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content pipeline running smoothly; team exploring expansion of scope and new initiatives (e.g., Reddit strategy)](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=836.0)
  - [Gary DeBerry is leaving Monograph; team discussing transition of newsletter responsibilities](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1135.0)
  - [Exploring potential for GrowthX to take over Monograph's newsletter production using existing HubSpot infrastructure](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1308.0)
  - [Discussing refinements to content strategy, including image creation and first-person writing style](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

## Topics

### Content Production Updates

  - [New batch of deliverables ready for review](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=41.0)
  - [Previously reviewed content to be published](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=49.0)
  - [Image creation process working well; defaulting to article titles as overlay text](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=55.0)
  - [Considering generating alternative headlines for images to increase engagement](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=241.0)
  - [First-person writing style generally working well, especially for Robert's byline](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=500.0)
  - [Team to keep monitoring for appropriateness of first-person usage](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=717.0)

### Reddit Content Strategy

  - [Proposal to create Monograph company Reddit account for Q\&A-style content](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=884.0)
  - [Goal: Improve visibility in LLM referrals, as Reddit is currently the top referrer](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
  - [Plan to develop a pipeline for generating Reddit-appropriate content](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=979.0)
  - [Initial focus on creating a spec document and rough pipeline for review](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1001.0)
  - [Aim for weekly cadence of posts to maintain natural, human-like presence](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1087.0)

### Newsletter Transition

  - [Robert inquired about GrowthX taking over Monograph's newsletter production](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
  - [Currently using HubSpot for lead scoring, marketing automation, and form fields](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1338.0)
  - [Need to ensure compatibility with existing systems (e.g., Chili Piper, app integrations)](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1346.0)
  - [Exploring potential for creating dedicated landing pages for newsletters](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1357.0)
  - [GrowthX to provide more detailed scope of work and capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1387.0)

### Content Strategy Refinements

  - [Discussing relevance of specific topics (e.g., ACH vs. EFT)](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1530.0)
  - [Considering creation of glossary or more targeted content clusters](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1570.0)
  - [Focus on balancing traffic generation with conversion potential](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1587.0)
  - [Exploring ways to incorporate financial terms in context relevant to A\&E firms](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1653.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to provide updated writing guidelines in next sync meeting](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=744.0)
  - [Develop preliminary spec for Reddit content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1001.0)
  - [GrowthX to gather and share information on newsletter production capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1387.0)
  - [Team to review flagged content in Airtable before final cleanup](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1496.0)
  - [Explore creation of more targeted financial content relevant to A\&E firms](https://fathom.video/share/V8ugc2LNc1KaQpXQCv13Bx8GMH1VKSEj?tab=summary&timestamp=1653.0)
